page: Vultr voc-g-24c-96gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a local storage configuration of 480 GB NVMe SSD. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests scoring 3208.88, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, scoring 74749.71 in stress-ng multi-core tests, which is in the top 25%. Memory performance is also notable, with a Passmark memory score of 2891.74 placing it in the top 25%, though large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain in the average tier at 126.87 GB/sec and 61.71 GB/sec respectively. For lightweight language model tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 342.31 tokens/second on a 135M model, while larger 7B model inference runs at average speeds.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential performance variability tradeoff compared to dedicated instances, though the high core count and NVMe storage mitigate resource density constraints. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced resource profile for multi-threaded applications without the premium of dedicated hardware. This server is configured for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and lightweight LLM text generation.

The instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is suitable for static web serving, database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and lightweight language model inference.
